title: casasia clusiifolia var. clusiifolia
intro: Casasia clusiifolia var. clusiifolia, also known as the Yellow Casasia or Yellow Pilocarpus, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rutaceae and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce fever and act as a diaphoretic, which helps to induce sweating and promote the elimination of toxins from the body. The health benefits of Casasia clusiifolia var. clusiifolia include its potential to alleviate symptoms of malaria, fever, and rheumatism, as well as its use as a treatment for digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Casasia clusiifolia var. clusiifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made from the plant's leaves and stems, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb.
